paper_title: Sediment Core Analysis and Chronology Development

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from a lake to study past environmental conditions.

steps.description: Sediment cores were collected in August 2014, following a bathymetric survey conducted with a Humminbird Matrix 47 3D sonar/GPS unit.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using sonar/GPS unit

steps.description: Cores were collected from a modular raft. Surface cores were collected with a Bolivia corer and stabilized with Zorbitrol.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface cores using Bolivia corer and stabilize

steps.description: Subsequent drives were extracted with a Livingstone corer.

methods.description: Logging and imaging of sediment cores to document and analyze their physical properties.

steps.description: Cores were logged and scanned at the National Lacustrine Core Facility (LacCore) in Minneapolis, Minnesota.
steps.searchable_summary: log and scan cores at LacCore

steps.description: High-resolution imaging was performed on a digital line scanner, producing an approximately 50 MB single image per 1.5-m core section at a resolution of 10 pixels per millimeter.

methods.description: Analysis of magnetic susceptibility of sediment cores to infer past environmental conditions.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility analyses were conducted on split cores with a Geotek MSCL-XYZ core scanner at a resolution of 0.5 cm.

methods.description: Grain size analysis of sediment samples to determine sediment composition and depositional environment.

steps.description: Grain size analysis was performed at the Arctic Coastal Geoscience Lab (ACGL) at the University of Alaska-Fairbanks (UAF) using a Beckman Coulter Counter LS 320.
steps.searchable_summary: perform grain size analysis using Beckman Coulter Counter

steps.description: Prior to analysis, organic matter was removed by oxidation. Samples (1 cm 3 ) were placed in approximately 10 mL of 30 percent hydrogen peroxide at room temperature until frothing ceased (~12-24 hours).

Samples were then washed in deionized water, centrifuged, decanted, and placed in approximately 10 mL of 1 percent sodium hexametaphosphate solution for approximately 24 hours prior to analysis to inhibit flocculation.

methods.description: Determination of organic content in sediment samples through loss on ignition.

steps.description: Loss on ignition (LOI, % organics) was conducted at the ACGL. One cubic centimeter of sediment was collected from core BBL4 every 1 cm from 470 to 425 cm and every 2 cm from 425 to 400 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples for LOI analysis

steps.description: We followed the standard ACGL lab procedure and heated samples for 1.5 hours at 550°C.

methods.description: Analysis of carbon and nitrogen stable isotopes in sediment samples to infer past environmental conditions.

steps.description: Ratios of carbon and nitrogen stable isotopes from bulk homogenized sediment samples were assessed using a ThermoFinnigan continuous-flow isotope ratio mass spectrometer at the UAF Alaska Stable Isotope Facility.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stable isotopes using mass spectrometer

methods.description: Analysis of pollen in sediment samples to reconstruct past vegetation and climate conditions.

steps.description: One cubic centimeter of sediment every 2 cm from 475 to 425 cm and every 4 cm from 425 to 400 cm was collected and processed for pollen analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and process sediment for pollen analysis

steps.description: Palynological processing at UAF followed a modified version of the standard methods described in Traverse (1988), including treatment with 10 percent HCl, 10 percent KOH, and HF to remove carbonates, humic acids, and silicates.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples with HCl, KOH, and HF

steps.description: Pollen diagrams were created in Tilia software, v1.7.16 (Grimm 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: create pollen diagrams using Tilia software

methods.description: Development of a chronological framework for sediment cores using radiocarbon dating and age-depth modeling.

steps.description: The chronology is based on radiocarbon dates of eight wood fragments from BBL4.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain radiocarbon dates for wood fragments

steps.description: Accelerator mass spectrometer ages were calibrated using IntCal13 with 2σ range.
The age-depth model was produced using Clam, v2.2.
